chapter_summary: Jesus returns to Capernaum: He forgives and heals a paralysed man (causing controversy about blasphemy), calls Matthew the tax collector, eats with sinners, answers fasting questions with new-wineskins teaching, heals the bleeding woman and raises Jairus's daughter, heals two blind men and a mute demoniac, then surveys the crowds with compassion ('sheep without a shepherd') and calls his disciples to pray for more workers.
main_lesson: Jesus came for people who know they need help — not for those who think they are already good enough. He calls sinners, heals the sick, raises the dead, and asks us to pray for more workers in His harvest.

memory_verse.kjv: But go ye and learn what that meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Go and learn what this means — God wants kindness, not just religious rules. I didn't come for people who think they're already fine. I came to find people who know they need Me.'

parent_guide: This chapter flows directly from chapter 8's power demonstrations into the question: who does Jesus use His power for? The paralysed man (v2-8) shows that forgiveness and healing are connected — Jesus heals the soul first. The calling of Matthew (v9) is striking: tax collectors were social pariahs and presumed traitors. The 'new wineskins' teaching (v16-17) is Jesus saying His Kingdom can't be squeezed into old religious containers. The two healings in vv18-26 are interlaced — Jairus's daughter and the bleeding woman — showing Jesus' compassion for the desperate. Verse 36 is a beautiful verse about Jesus' heart: 'he was moved with compassion.'
application_for_children: Jesus came for everyone — even people who feel left out or unloved. When we feel like that, we can remember: Jesus especially came for us.

verses.kjv: And he entered into a ship, and passed over, and came into his own city.
verses.little_bible: Jesus got into the boat and crossed back over the lake to Capernaum, the town where He often stayed. He was always on the move, bringing the Kingdom wherever He went.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus crossed the lake and came back to His own town.
verses.meaning: After being asked to leave the Gadarene region, Jesus returned to Capernaum — which Matthew calls 'his own city'. Jesus does not force Himself on those who reject Him, but He keeps going to those who will receive Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for coming back again and again. Even when I fail, You don't give up on me.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus left when people asked Him to. What does that tell you about how He treats people's choices?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus returned to where people wanted Him. Is Jesus welcome in your home and in your daily life?
verses.do_it_today: Invite Jesus into your home by praying together as a family before you start your day.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus stepping out of a boat onto a dock, townspeople welcoming Him, a warm town in the background.

verses.kjv: And, behold, they brought to him a man sick of the palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy; Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.
verses.little_bible: People carried a paralysed man to Jesus on a mat. When Jesus saw how much they all believed, He looked at the man and said, 'Son, cheer up — your sins are forgiven!' Jesus saw the need underneath the need.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Friends brought a paralysed man to Jesus. Jesus said, 'Your sins are forgiven!'
verses.meaning: Jesus addresses the man's spiritual condition before his physical one. 'Son' is a term of warm affection. He also acknowledged the faith of those who brought him — intercession matters.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for forgiving my sins. That is the best kind of healing. Help me believe it.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus forgave the man's sins before He healed his body?
verses.family_discussion: Real friends bring people to Jesus. Who in your life can you bring closer to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Thank Jesus specifically for forgiving your sins — the healing that lasts forever.
verses.illustration_prompt: Four friends lowering a mat toward Jesus, the paralysed man looking up at Jesus with wonder and hope.

verses.kjv: And, behold, certain of the scribes said within themselves, This man blasphemeth.
verses.little_bible: Some of the religious teachers muttered to themselves, 'This man is blaspheming! Only God can forgive sins!' They were right about one thing — only God can forgive sins.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some religious teachers thought, 'Only God can forgive sins!'
verses.meaning: The scribes understood the implication of Jesus' words perfectly: only God can forgive sins. Their accusation of blasphemy was logical — unless Jesus really was God. That is exactly who He claimed to be.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe You are God — and that You truly have the power to forgive me. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: The scribes said only God can forgive sins — and they were right. What does that tell you about who Jesus is?
verses.family_discussion: The scribes were offended because they did not accept who Jesus was. How does believing Jesus is truly God change how we live?
verses.do_it_today: Write down: 'Jesus is God. Jesus has forgiven me.' Put it somewhere you will see it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Robed religious leaders whispering angrily to each other in the corner while Jesus speaks to the paralysed man.

verses.kjv: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?
verses.little_bible: Jesus knew exactly what they were thinking — even though they hadn't said it out loud. He turned to them and asked, 'Why are you thinking evil things in your hearts?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus knew their thoughts and asked, 'Why are you thinking evil?'
verses.meaning: Jesus' ability to know their unspoken thoughts is itself a divine attribute. He does not ignore silent opposition — He brings it into the light gently but directly.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You know what I'm thinking even before I say it. Help my thoughts be clean and good.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus knew what the scribes were thinking without them saying it. Does that make you think about what you think about?
verses.family_discussion: Our hearts are open before God — even our private thoughts. How does that change how you think about your 'secret' attitudes?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to show you one thought pattern that doesn't please Him and ask Him to help you change it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus turning to the scribes with gentle but piercing eyes, the scribes suddenly looking uncomfortable.

verses.kjv: For whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked them a question: 'Which is easier — to say your sins are forgiven, or to tell someone to get up and walk?' It takes the same power to do both!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked, 'Which is easier — forgive sins or say get up and walk?'
verses.meaning: Jesus poses a clever question: either statement is easy to say, but the physical healing is visible and verifiable in a way forgiveness is not. He will prove His authority to forgive by doing the visible miracle.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You for giving us real proof that You have power — even power to forgive.
verses.discussion_question: Can you test whether someone's sins have been forgiven? How is it different from testing whether someone can walk?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus linked two kinds of healing. Why is forgiveness even more important than physical healing?
verses.do_it_today: Read verses 5 and 6 together and explain them to a younger sibling or family member.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing with open hands toward the scribes and then toward the paralysed man, presenting His logical argument clearly.

verses.kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (then saith he to the sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'So that you will know the Son of Man has power to forgive sins on earth —' Then He turned to the paralysed man and said, 'Get up, pick up your mat, and go home!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said to the paralysed man, 'Get up, take your mat, and go home!'
verses.meaning: Jesus performs the visible miracle as evidence of His invisible authority. The physical healing is a 'sign' pointing to a greater spiritual reality: He has power to forgive sin itself.
verses.memory_phrase: The Son of Man forgives sins
verses.prayer: Jesus, Son of Man, You have all power on earth and in heaven. I trust You with my whole life.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus healed the man to prove He could forgive sins. Which healing do you think was greater?
verses.family_discussion: Everything Jesus did was connected to a deeper purpose. How can your family look for the deeper meaning in what Jesus does in your lives?
verses.do_it_today: Stand up from where you are sitting and say out loud: 'Jesus has the power to forgive, and I am forgiven!'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus pointing firmly and lovingly at the paralysed man on the mat, the man beginning to push himself upright with eyes wide in wonder.

verses.kjv: And he arose, and departed to his house.
verses.little_bible: And the man got up! He picked up his mat and walked home. Just like that. Jesus had said it, and it happened.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man stood up, picked up his mat, and walked home!
verses.meaning: The miracle is complete and simple. No special effects, no long recovery. Jesus said it; the man was healed. The brevity of this verse emphasises how immediate and total the miracle was.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your words have power to change everything instantly. Speak into my life today.
verses.discussion_question: The man got up immediately when Jesus spoke. What would you do if Jesus healed you right then and there?
verses.family_discussion: Imagine how that man felt walking home on legs that had not worked. What would you want to do first?
verses.do_it_today: Walk around the room and thank God for your body — and for the healing Jesus brings.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man carrying a rolled-up mat under his arm, walking confidently and joyfully down a street, people staring in amazement.

verses.kjv: But when the multitudes saw it, they marvelled, and glorified God, which had given such power unto men.
verses.little_bible: When the crowd saw the man get up and walk, they were amazed — and they gave glory to God! They worshipped God for giving such amazing power to Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The crowd was amazed and praised God for Jesus' power.
verses.meaning: The crowd's response was right and proper: they glorified God. Even if they did not yet understand the full extent of who Jesus was, they recognised that God was at work through Him.
verses.prayer: God, when I see something amazing, help me remember to give You the glory. You are the source of all good things.
verses.discussion_question: The crowd glorified God when they saw the miracle. When something good happens, do you remember to thank God?
verses.family_discussion: Praising God together is one of the most important things a family can do. What has God done recently that deserves your praise?
verses.do_it_today: Think of three things God has done this week and praise Him for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: A crowd of people with hands raised and faces upturned in wonder and worship, the healed man standing tall among them.

verses.kjv: And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Matthew, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.
verses.little_bible: As Jesus walked on, He saw a man named Matthew sitting at a tax booth collecting money. Jesus simply said, 'Follow Me.' And Matthew stood up and followed Him — right away!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw Matthew the tax collector and said, 'Follow Me.' And he did!
verses.meaning: Tax collectors were despised as traitors — they worked for Rome and often cheated their own people. Jesus chose one as a disciple. This is radical grace. Matthew's immediate response — leaving his lucrative post — shows the power of Jesus' call.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for calling people that others give up on. Thank You for calling me.
verses.discussion_question: Tax collectors were hated. Why do you think Jesus chose Matthew?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus saw Matthew and called him before Matthew changed his life. Do you ever wait to be 'good enough' before coming to Jesus? What does this story say about that?
verses.do_it_today: Is there someone you have given up on? Ask God to help you see them the way Jesus saw Matthew.
verses.illustration_prompt: Matthew sitting at a stone tax booth with coins stacked before him, looking up with wide eyes as Jesus extends His hand in invitation.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as Jesus sat at meat in the house, behold, many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.
verses.little_bible: Later, Jesus was eating at Matthew's house. Lots of tax collectors and other people with bad reputations came and sat down to eat with Jesus and His disciples. Jesus welcomed them all.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus ate with lots of tax collectors and sinners at Matthew's house.
verses.meaning: Matthew celebrated his new life by throwing a party — and his social circle was the 'wrong crowd'. Jesus did not hold back. He sat with them, ate with them, and welcomed them. Table fellowship was deeply significant in that culture.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus welcomes everyone to His table
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for welcoming me — even when I feel like I'm not good enough. Help me welcome others too.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus ate with people others looked down on. Who are the 'wrong crowd' today, and how should we treat them?
verses.family_discussion: Matthew's first response to following Jesus was to bring his friends to meet Jesus. How can you bring your circle of friends closer to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Invite someone your family hasn't spent time with to share a meal or snack together.
verses.illustration_prompt: A busy, cheerful dinner table with Jesus at the centre, surrounded by a diverse, rough-looking crowd eating and listening with joy.

verses.kjv: And when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto his disciples, Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?
verses.little_bible: When the Pharisees — the strict religious leaders — saw this, they complained to Jesus' disciples: 'Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and terrible sinners?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Pharisees complained, 'Why does Jesus eat with sinners?'
verses.meaning: The Pharisees used meals as a sorting system: eating with someone meant accepting them. To eat with sinners was, in their view, to endorse sinfulness. But Jesus was operating by a different logic entirely.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus eats with sinners by design
verses.prayer: Lord, help me not look down on people who are still finding their way to You. Help me see them like You do.
verses.discussion_question: The Pharisees were upset that Jesus ate with 'bad' people. Do you ever avoid certain people? Why?
verses.family_discussion: Religious people sometimes build walls while Jesus builds tables. How does your family decide who is 'in' and who is 'out'?
verses.do_it_today: Make a deliberate effort to be kind to someone you normally ignore or avoid.
verses.illustration_prompt: Pharisees in fine robes standing at a distance, pointing accusingly at the dinner table where Jesus sits with the crowd, disapproval on their faces.

verses.kjv: But when Jesus heard that, he said unto them, They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.
verses.little_bible: Jesus heard them and answered, 'Healthy people don't need a doctor — sick people do.' He was saying: He came for the people who know they need help.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Sick people need a doctor — I came for those who need help.'
verses.meaning: Jesus reframes the complaint beautifully: a doctor belongs with the sick. His presence among sinners was not compromise — it was mission. He is the Great Physician and He goes where the need is greatest.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I know I need You. I am the sick person who needs the doctor. Thank You for coming for me.
verses.discussion_question: If you were very sick, would you want a doctor to stay away from you to keep their reputation clean?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was the doctor going to the sick. Who in your world is spiritually unwell and needs someone to bring Jesus to them?
verses.do_it_today: Write a prayer for one person in your life who needs 'the Great Physician' right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus as a warm, approachable healer figure surrounded by people reaching out for help, Pharisees watching from afar.

verses.kjv: But go ye and learn what that meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Go and learn what this means — God wants kindness, not just religious rules. I didn't come for people who think they're already fine. I came to find people who know they need Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I came not for the 'good' people but to call sinners to come back to God.'
verses.meaning: Jesus quotes Hosea 6:6 — 'I desire mercy, not sacrifice.' Religion without compassion misses the heart of God. Jesus came precisely for those who recognise their need. 'Repentance' means turning back to God.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I am the sinner You came for. Thank You for finding me and calling me to come back to God.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'know you need Jesus'? Do you know that about yourself?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus quotes Hosea: God wants mercy more than religious performance. What is the difference between being religious and having a real relationship with Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Look up Hosea 6:6 and read it together as a family.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking with warmth and authority, hand extended toward a group of ordinary, humble-looking people who look relieved and welcome.

verses.kjv: Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?
verses.little_bible: John the Baptist's followers came to Jesus with a question: 'Why do we and the Pharisees fast a lot, but your disciples never fast?' They wanted to understand Jesus' way.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John's disciples asked, 'Why don't your disciples fast like we do?'
verses.meaning: Fasting was a regular practice of devout Jews and John's disciples. Jesus' disciples seemed to stand out by not practising it — at least not in the same way. This question leads to one of Jesus' most memorable teaching images.
verses.memory_phrase: Different seasons call for different responses
verses.prayer: Lord, help me understand what season I am in with You and how to respond to You well in it.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever wondered why different Christians do things differently? What is most important about how we follow Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: John's disciples were sincere but didn't fully understand what Jesus was doing yet. How do we ask honest questions about faith without judging others?
verses.do_it_today: Ask someone older in your faith a sincere question about how they follow Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: John's disciples — slightly austere-looking, with simple clothing — approaching Jesus with a curious, earnest expression.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered, 'You don't mourn at a wedding while the groom is still there! But the day will come when I am taken away — and then they will fast.' Jesus was talking about His death.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'You don't fast at a wedding! But one day I will be gone, and then they will.'
verses.meaning: Jesus uses the image of a wedding feast — a symbol of great joy. His presence with the disciples was a season of rejoicing. He was pointing, quietly, toward His coming death when fasting would be fitting.
verses.memory_phrase: Rejoice while the Groom is here
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are with me now through Your Spirit. Help me celebrate Your presence every day.
verses.discussion_question: What image does Jesus use to describe Himself? What does it mean that Jesus is like a bridegroom?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus called His time on earth a wedding-feast season. One day He will return and the real wedding feast begins. How does that hope change how you live?
verses.do_it_today: Celebrate Jesus today — play worship music, draw something joyful, or sing a praise song together.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful wedding scene with guests celebrating around a table, the groom in the centre — a gentle picture of what Jesus was describing.

verses.kjv: No man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Nobody sews a brand-new patch on an old worn-out coat — the new patch will pull away and make an even bigger tear!' New things don't just fit neatly into old things.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: New cloth on an old coat just makes the tear worse.
verses.meaning: Jesus is explaining that His Kingdom cannot simply be patched onto the old religious system. The two are incompatible. He is not updating Judaism's rules — He is bringing something entirely new.
verses.prayer: Jesus, make me new from the inside — don't just patch the old me. Change my heart completely.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you sew new fabric onto very old cloth? Why is Jesus saying this?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus brought a completely new covenant, not just an improved old one. How has following Jesus changed your family — not just added some religious habits?
verses.do_it_today: Think about one habit in your life. Is it a real change or just a new patch on old ways?
verses.illustration_prompt: A tailor looking frustrated at a coat that has torn apart where a new bright patch was sewn onto old, worn fabric.

verses.kjv: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued, 'And nobody puts new wine into old cracked wineskins. The new wine will burst the old skins and everything will be ruined. New wine needs new wineskins — then both are kept safe!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: New wine needs new wineskins — or everything breaks.
verses.meaning: Old wineskins (dried leather) cannot expand with the fermenting new wine. Jesus' Kingdom life — vibrant, alive, expanding — requires hearts and structures flexible enough to hold it. The new covenant is not a patch or an addition — it is transformation.
verses.prayer: Lord, make my heart a new wineskin — flexible, fresh, and ready to hold Your life.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think Jesus means when He says we need to be a 'new wineskin'?
verses.family_discussion: Old wineskins were rigid and inflexible. How can a family or church become so rigid that God's work can't grow in them?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God: 'Is there something rigid in my heart that needs to change so Your life can grow in me?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A cracked, dried wineskin splitting apart as new wine bubbles out, contrasted with a fresh, supple new wineskin holding the wine safely.

verses.kjv: While he spake these things unto them, behold, there came a certain ruler, and worshipped him, saying, My daughter is even now dead: but come and lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.
verses.little_bible: Right while Jesus was still speaking, a synagogue leader fell before Him in worship and said, 'My daughter has just died — but if You come and put Your hand on her, she will live again!' He believed the impossible.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A ruler fell before Jesus: 'My daughter just died — but come and she will live!'
verses.meaning: Jairus' faith is extraordinary: his daughter was already dead, yet he believed Jesus could still help. His worship shows his heart before his words. This is faith that reaches past death.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me have faith like Jairus — to believe You can do the impossible even when all hope seems gone.
verses.discussion_question: Jairus' daughter had already died, but he still believed Jesus could help. Have you ever prayed for something that seemed impossible?
verses.family_discussion: Jairus was a religious leader, yet he fell at Jesus' feet. Humility before Jesus is the beginning of seeing His power.
verses.do_it_today: Kneel when you pray tonight as a family — a physical reminder of coming before Jesus in humility.
verses.illustration_prompt: A well-dressed synagogue leader prostrating himself before Jesus in anguish and faith, the crowd watching in surprise.

verses.kjv: And Jesus arose, and followed him, and so did his disciples.
verses.little_bible: Jesus immediately got up and went with him. The disciples followed too. When someone needs Jesus, He goes.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus got up at once and followed the ruler. The disciples came too.
verses.meaning: Jesus responds without deliberation or delay. He simply rises and goes. His willingness to be interrupted, to change plans, to go to someone in crisis is a picture of His heart.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You don't hesitate when I need You. You always come.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus dropped everything to go with Jairus. What does that tell you about how important you are to Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus let Himself be interrupted. How good is your family at stopping what you're doing when someone really needs you?
verses.do_it_today: Practice being interruptible today — stop and really listen when someone in your family needs you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing and stepping forward immediately after the kneeling ruler, disciples quickly falling in behind them.

verses.kjv: And, behold, a woman, which was diseased with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment:
verses.little_bible: As they walked, a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years crept through the crowd. She came up behind Jesus and quietly touched the edge of His robe.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A woman who had been sick for twelve years sneaked up to touch Jesus' robe.
verses.meaning: This woman was ceremonially unclean for twelve years — a condition that meant isolation from worship and community. Her reaching through the crowd to touch Jesus was an act of desperate, courageous faith.
verses.memory_phrase: She reached through the crowd to Jesus
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me push through whatever is in the way and reach for You, just like this woman did.
verses.discussion_question: This woman had been sick for twelve years. How do you think she felt when she decided to try one more time?
verses.family_discussion: She didn't think she deserved to speak to Jesus — she just wanted to touch the edge of His robe. Do you ever feel too small or too broken to approach Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Whatever has been making you feel 'unqualified' to come to Jesus — bring it to Him today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A frail, determined woman pushing gently through a crowd, fingers just reaching the fringe of Jesus' robe as He walks ahead.

verses.kjv: For she said within herself, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be whole.
verses.little_bible: She had been saying to herself, 'If I can just touch even the edge of His clothes, I know I will be completely healed.' Her whole faith rested in one small touch.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: She thought, 'If I just touch His clothes, I will be healed!'
verses.meaning: Her faith was private and specific — she had a clear mental picture of what she believed would happen. Faith is often like this: not a vague hope but a specific, directed trust.
verses.prayer: Lord, I want faith like hers — quiet, specific, and completely trusting. Even one small step toward You is enough.
verses.discussion_question: She believed that just touching Jesus' clothes would heal her. How simple does faith have to be to work?
verses.family_discussion: Faith doesn't have to be dramatic or loud. This woman prayed silently in a crowd. How can you encourage quiet, personal faith in your family?
verses.do_it_today: Pray quietly to Jesus today about one specific thing — just you and Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Close-up of a hand reaching out, fingertips just brushing a white robe, a look of determined hope on the woman's face.

verses.kjv: But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, be of good cheer; thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.
verses.little_bible: Jesus stopped, turned around, and looked right at her. He said, 'Daughter, be encouraged — your faith has made you whole!' And from that moment, she was completely healed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw her and said, 'Daughter, your faith healed you!' She was made whole.
verses.meaning: Jesus stops mid-journey to address this woman. He calls her 'Daughter' — a term of loving belonging. He attributes her healing to her faith. She is not just physically healed but restored to belonging.
verses.memory_phrase: Daughter — your faith made you whole
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for stopping for me. Thank You that my faith — however small — matters to You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus called her 'Daughter.' What does it mean to be called a child of Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus stopped for this woman even while rushing to a dying girl. No one is too small or too unimportant to receive Jesus' attention. How does that make you feel?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone in your family: 'You matter. You are seen.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus turning from the crowd with warm, seeing eyes, locking gaze with the healed woman who kneels before Him with tears of relief and joy.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus came into the ruler's house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,
verses.little_bible: When Jesus arrived at Jairus' house, He found professional mourners and a noisy crowd of people wailing. They were already acting as if all hope was gone.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: At the ruler's house, mourners were already crying and making noise.
verses.meaning: Professional mourners were customary at Jewish funerals. Their presence and noise signalled that hope was completely over. Jesus was walking into the middle of declared despair.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus walks into our hopeless places
verses.prayer: Jesus, walk into the places in my life where hope feels gone. You are the God of impossible hope.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think it was like for Jairus to walk back into his house with all those mourners there?
verses.family_discussion: There will be times when everything feels hopeless. How does your family hold onto faith in those moments?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one 'hopeless' situation and write next to it: 'Jesus can walk into this.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A house full of wailing people in mourning clothes, flutes playing, a scene of declared grief — as Jesus approaches the door.

verses.kjv: He said, Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them all to go outside and said, 'The girl is not dead — she is only sleeping.' They laughed at Him. They thought He didn't know what He was talking about.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'She is only sleeping.' And the crowd laughed at Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus' statement was not denial of death but a declaration of intent: in His eyes, a death He was about to reverse was already as temporary as sleep. The crowd's mockery only underlines the impossibility He was about to overcome.
verses.memory_phrase: What looks dead to us is sleeping to Jesus
verses.prayer: Jesus, what looks hopeless to me is not hopeless to You. Help me see through Your eyes.
verses.discussion_question: People laughed at Jesus. How did He respond? What do you think that teaches us about what people think versus what Jesus knows?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd laughed because they had given up. Are there situations in your family or community where you've given up too quickly?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to show you one situation you have given up on — and then ask Him if He has given up on it too.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing calmly in the centre of the room as mourners laugh and mock, His expression steady and kind.

verses.kjv: But when the people were put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand, and the maid arose.
verses.little_bible: Jesus sent everyone outside. Then He went in, took the girl's hand — and she got up. That simply. That completely. The dead girl was alive.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus took the girl's hand and she rose up — alive!
verses.meaning: Jesus' touch brings life. He takes the girl by the hand — the same gentle, personal gesture He used with the leper, with Peter's mother-in-law. Jesus is never clinical. He is always personal.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus took her hand and she arose
verses.prayer: Jesus, take my hand too. Where I am weak or cold inside, bring me back to life.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus sent the mockers away before doing the miracle. Why do you think He did that?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus raised this girl privately and gently. Why do you think He is sometimes quieter in His biggest miracles?
verses.do_it_today: Hold someone's hand today and pray a blessing over them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gently holding the hand of a young girl lying on a mat, her eyes beginning to open, warm light filling the room.

verses.kjv: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.
verses.little_bible: And news of what Jesus had done spread to every part of the region. You cannot keep a resurrection quiet!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: News of this miracle spread everywhere.
verses.meaning: Despite Jesus sometimes asking people not to tell (see chapter 8), this news spread naturally and widely. The raising of the dead is a category of miracle that points unmistakably to the Messiah.
verses.prayer: Lord, let the news of what You have done spread from my mouth too. Give me courage to tell my story.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think this news spread so quickly?
verses.family_discussion: The news of Jesus raising the dead spread everywhere. How does your family spread the news of what Jesus has done?
verses.do_it_today: Share one thing Jesus has done in your family with someone outside your home today.
verses.illustration_prompt: People rushing from house to house, faces alight with amazement, the news visibly spreading through a town.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed him, crying, and saying, Thou son of David, have mercy on us.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus left that place, two blind men followed Him, calling out loudly, 'Son of David, have mercy on us!' They used a royal title for the Messiah — they knew who Jesus was.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Two blind men followed Jesus crying, 'Son of David, have mercy on us!'
verses.meaning: 'Son of David' is a messianic title — these men were declaring their belief that Jesus was the promised King-Messiah of Israel. Their persistence ('followed') underlines the seriousness of their faith.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me too. I need Your help just as much as they did.
verses.discussion_question: The blind men called Jesus 'Son of David.' What did they mean by that title?
verses.family_discussion: These men could not see Jesus — but they knew who He was. How can we 'see' Jesus even when we can't see Him with our eyes?
verses.do_it_today: Find a quiet place and call out to Jesus — use your voice, not just your thoughts.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two men with clouded, unseeing eyes, arms outstretched, following the sound of Jesus' footsteps, calling out with hope.

verses.kjv: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.
verses.little_bible: When they reached the house, Jesus asked them directly, 'Do you believe I am able to do this?' They said, 'Yes, Lord!' Jesus always asked about faith — because faith is the door.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked, 'Do you believe I can do this?' They said, 'Yes, Lord!'
verses.meaning: Before healing them, Jesus draws out their faith explicitly. His question is not because He needs information — it is to give them the opportunity to declare what they believe. Faith is meant to be spoken.
verses.memory_phrase: Do you believe I am able?
verses.prayer: Yes, Lord, I believe You are able! Help my belief grow bigger every day.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus asked 'Do you believe I can?' What is the right answer to that question when you come to Jesus with a need?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus asked their faith out loud before healing. Spoken faith is powerful. How does your family speak faith together, not just think it?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'I believe Jesus is able to ____________.' Fill in the blank with your real need.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling to the eye level of the two blind men, looking at them with a direct, searching, kind gaze.

verses.kjv: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith be it unto you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus touched their eyes and said, 'Let it happen to you just as you believed.' Your faith opened the door, and Jesus walked through it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus touched their eyes: 'Be it done according to your faith!'
verses.meaning: Jesus touches their eyes — again, always personal. And He links the measure of the miracle to the measure of their faith. Faith is not magic — but it is the channel through which God's power flows into our lives.
verses.prayer: Lord, grow my faith so that more of Your power can flow through my life and into the world.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said it would happen 'according to your faith.' How does this make you want to grow your faith?
verses.family_discussion: Faith matters. How does your family build faith together — through the Bible, through prayer, through sharing stories of what God has done?
verses.do_it_today: Share a testimony of something God has done in your family to build each other's faith.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus placing both thumbs gently on the closed eyes of the two men, a warm glow emanating from His hands.

verses.kjv: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus straitly charged them, saying, See that no man know it.
verses.little_bible: Their eyes opened! They could see! Jesus told them firmly, 'Make sure you don't tell anyone about this.' He was not looking for fame — He was doing it out of love.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Their eyes opened! Jesus told them firmly, 'Don't tell anyone.'
verses.meaning: Jesus frequently instructed those He healed to stay quiet — likely to prevent premature public confrontation and to keep His focus on those still to be reached. His miracles were acts of compassion, not publicity.
verses.memory_phrase: Healed for love, not for fame
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me serve and give without needing people to notice. Let me be like You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus told them not to tell anyone. Why might Jesus have said that?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus did not do His miracles to build His own reputation. How does that challenge the way we often want people to notice the good we do?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing today without telling anyone about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two men standing with eyes wide open and sparkling with wonder, Jesus placing a finger to His lips with a warm, serious expression.

verses.kjv: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad his fame in all that country.
verses.little_bible: But the men left and told everyone — the news of what Jesus had done spread all across the country. When Jesus does something wonderful in your life, it is very hard to stay quiet!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: But they left and told everyone about Jesus anyway!
verses.meaning: They disobeyed the instruction — but their motive was joy and gratitude, not defiance. The unstoppable spreading of news about Jesus is a recurring theme: His works could not be contained.
verses.prayer: Jesus, fill my heart with such gratitude for what You have done that I can't help but tell people.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think they couldn't keep quiet even when Jesus asked them to?
verses.family_discussion: They were overflowing with gratitude. Is your family overflowing with gratitude for what Jesus has done?
verses.do_it_today: List five things Jesus has done for your family and put the list on your fridge.
verses.illustration_prompt: The two formerly blind men walking joyfully through a crowd, eyes bright, arms open, visibly unable to contain their excitement.

verses.kjv: As they went out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a devil.
verses.little_bible: Just as the blind men were leaving, people brought Jesus a man who could not speak at all because of an evil spirit. The needs kept coming — and Jesus kept helping.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Next, people brought a man who could not speak because of a demon.
verses.meaning: The healings continue without pause. Matthew stacks miracle after miracle in these chapters to make a cumulative point: no need is too great, too strange, or too frequent for Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus never runs out of compassion
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your compassion never runs out. Whatever I bring to You, You have more than enough for me.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus was surrounded by need all day long. How do you think He felt? What kept Him going?
verses.family_discussion: Compassion fatigue is real for humans. But Jesus never tired of helping. What can your family do to have more of Jesus' kind of compassion?
verses.do_it_today: Look for one person today whose need you have been ignoring and do something about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: People gently leading a silent, downcast man toward Jesus just as the healed blind men walk away in the background.

verses.kjv: And when the devil was cast out, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus cast out the evil spirit, the man spoke! The crowds were astonished and said, 'Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel before!' Jesus was doing things nobody had ever seen.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus cast out the demon and the man spoke! The crowd said, 'Nothing like this has ever happened!'
verses.meaning: The crowd's response confirms the uniqueness of Jesus' ministry. He was not just a healer in a long line of healers — He was doing something historically unprecedented.
verses.prayer: Jesus, there is no one like You — ever. I worship You for being completely one of a kind.
verses.discussion_question: The crowd said they had never seen anything like this. What is the most amazing thing you know about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: People who had seen many teachers and prophets said Jesus was different from all of them. What makes Jesus uniquely different from everyone else?
verses.do_it_today: Draw or write one thing that makes Jesus completely unique and special to you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man opening his mouth wide in speech for the first time, his face transformed with wonder, the crowd around him staring in amazement.

verses.kjv: But the Pharisees said, He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.
verses.little_bible: But the Pharisees said, 'He only casts out demons because the ruler of demons gives Him the power!' They were so determined not to believe that they said something completely backwards.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Pharisees said, 'He uses the devil's power.' They refused to believe.
verses.meaning: The Pharisees' accusation is illogical — why would Satan cast out Satan? But it shows that hardened hearts can find an explanation for anything rather than submit to truth. Witnessing miracles alone does not produce faith.
verses.prayer: Lord, keep my heart open to truth. Don't let me make excuses to avoid what You are showing me.
verses.discussion_question: The Pharisees saw the exact same miracles the crowd did — but came to a completely different conclusion. Why?
verses.family_discussion: Seeing is not always believing. What is it that opens a heart to truly see Jesus for who He is?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to keep your heart soft and open to His truth today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of Pharisees huddle together at the edge of the crowd, arms crossed, frowning, talking among themselves dismissively.

verses.kjv: And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.
verses.little_bible: Jesus kept travelling to every town and village. He taught in the synagogues, preached the Good News about God's Kingdom, and healed every kind of sickness. He left no one out.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to every city, teaching, preaching, and healing everyone.
verses.meaning: Matthew gives a summary statement of Jesus' entire ministry pattern: teach, preach, heal. He went everywhere — not just to the important places. His ministry was comprehensive and his compassion universal.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your good news is for every person in every place. Help me bring Your Kingdom wherever I go.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus went to every city and village. What kind of person do you think He would come to visit if He were walking around today?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus modelled a three-part ministry: teaching, preaching, healing. How does your family participate in any of these with the people around you?
verses.do_it_today: Teach one person something from the Bible today, no matter how small.
verses.illustration_prompt: A map-like bird's eye view of rolling hills and villages with a figure of Jesus moving from town to town, a warm light following His path.

verses.kjv: But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus looked out at the crowds, His heart broke for them. They were exhausted, lost, and scattered — like sheep with nobody to lead them. Jesus looked at people and felt their pain.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus felt deep compassion for the crowds — they were like lost sheep with no shepherd.
verses.meaning: 'Moved with compassion' — in Greek, this is one of the strongest emotion words in the language. It describes a gut-wrenching reaction, not just a surface feeling. Jesus saw the crowds and was physically affected by their lostness.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is moved by our lostness
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for seeing me even in the crowd and feeling for me. Help me have Your compassion for others.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus saw the crowd and felt it in His gut. What kinds of people in your life make you feel compassion like that?
verses.family_discussion: Compassion means to 'suffer with' someone. How can your family practise truly feeling with people instead of just watching from a distance?
verses.do_it_today: Find one person today who seems lost or lonely and spend five minutes with them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus on a hillside looking out over a vast, wandering crowd of tired, scattered people, His face full of deep, aching love.

verses.kjv: Then saith he unto his disciples, The harvest truly is plenteous, but the labourers are few;
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to His disciples, 'There is a huge harvest waiting — so many people who need to hear the Good News. But there are not nearly enough workers to bring it in!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'The harvest is huge but the workers are few!'
verses.meaning: Jesus shifts from the image of sheep to the image of harvest — both speak of abundance waiting for response. The harvest is ready; the problem is insufficient labourers. The solution is not panic but prayer.
verses.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to the harvest around me. I want to be one of Your workers.
verses.discussion_question: What is the 'harvest' Jesus is talking about? Who are the workers?
verses.family_discussion: There are more people who need Jesus in the world today than ever. How does your family participate in bringing in the harvest?
verses.do_it_today: Look around your neighbourhood or school. Who do you know that hasn't heard about Jesus?
verses.illustration_prompt: Rolling golden fields of wheat stretching to the horizon, ripe and ready, with only a few small figures working in the distance.

verses.kjv: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'So ask the Lord of the harvest — ask God — to send more workers out into His fields!' The first answer to the problem of the world's need is to pray.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Pray that God will send more workers into His harvest!'
verses.meaning: Jesus' solution to the worker shortage is prayer — not guilt, not effort alone, but crying out to the Lord of the harvest. This is the foundation of all mission: it must begin with prayer.
verses.prayer: Lord of the harvest, please send more workers! And Lord — here I am. Please send me too.
verses.discussion_question: Why does Jesus say the first step is to pray, not just to do more?
verses.family_discussion: Prayer is the engine of mission. How does your family pray for the world — for places where people haven't heard about Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Pray together as a family for three people in your life who don't yet know Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A family on their knees together in prayer, the golden harvest fields visible through the window behind them, waiting.
